title: Floor in a Binary Search Tree

content.problemStatement:
Given a Binary Search Tree and a key, return the floor of the given key in the Binary Search Tree.

Floor of a value refers to the value of the largest node in the Binary Search Tree that is smaller than or equal to the given key.
If the floor node does not exist, return nullptr.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the tree <= 10^5
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a binary search tree and a key. The binary search tree will be represented as a tree where each node has a value and two children (left and right). The key will be an integer.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the floor of the given key in the binary search tree. If the floor node does not exist, return null.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: We will use a recursive approach to traverse the binary search tree and find the floor of the given key.
solution.timeComplexity: O(h) where h is the height of the tree
solution.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is the height of the tree
